rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PETERSON et al VS. ALFONSO CASTRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For An Order Deeming Req For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 10, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ANDREW PETERSON, SUSAN PETERSON Motion For An Order Deeming Request For Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted in part and denied in part. As relevant here, the only persons who can claim the attorney-client privilege are the client and the person who was the lawyer of the client at the time of such confidential communications. Evidence Code section 954. The pleadings are not clear as to whether or not Zach Georgopoulos or his firm was the attorney for SC Industry Group, Inc. or SCIG Impala, LLC, and, if so, whether Mr. Castro was the person authorized by those entities to claim the privilege. Accordingly, the Motion is granted as to RFA's 1-7; 20-38 and those Requests are deemed admitted. The Motion is denied as to RFA's 8-11. The Court does not have sufficient information to rule on RFA's 12-19, but encourages the parties to meet and confer as to these RFA's with the knowledge that Evidence Code section 954(c) will be applied by the Court if the parties are unable to resolve the issue themselves. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES W. GAY VS. CREATIVE ALLIANCE, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Danilo Design Group, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc. And Wb Coyle'S Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 10, 2014, Line 13, Defendants Danilo Design Group, Telegraph Hill Properties Inc. And Wb Coyle'S Motion For A Protective Order Regarding Depositions Defendant's motion for a protective order limiting the questioning of WB Coyle as an individual and person most knowledgeable of Danilo Design Group, L.P. and Telegraph Hill Properties, Inc., is denied. Said defendants failed to timely serve written objections to the deposition notices and failed to timely file their motion for a protective order prior to the dates set for commencement of questioning. The deposition testimony of WB Coyle two years prior to the filing of the instant action as a witness in an arbitration proceeding which has since been dismissed has no relationship to his deposition testimony as a party the within action and person most knowledgeable of Danilo Design Group and Telephone Hill Properties. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER BAKER VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Evidence
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 10, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS DIGNITY HEALTH, A CORPORATION, ADELWISA MAXIE, GLORIA LAU and SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication; Evidence Motion for summary judgment or adjudication is denied. As to the defamation and false light causes of action, triable issues of material fact exist. First, as to the email sent by Defendant Adelwisa Maxie to four union members, triable issues of material fact as to Maxie's motivation for sending the email. Based on the plain language of the email, a reasonable jury could find that Maxie sent the email to vent about her feeling and experience to her colleagues, and would therefore not be entitled to the common interest privilege. (UMF 144-145, 147, 166.) Additionally, triable issues of material fact remain as to whether Defendants' failure to conduct an investigation as to who left the voicemail amounts to recklessness. (UMF 124, 126, 128-136.) "The mere profession of a defendant that he believed in good faith that his statements were true does not automatically entitle him to a verdict in his favor¿.[r]eckless is, after all, only negligence raised to a higher power¿[f]urther actual malice may be inferred when the investigation was grossly inadequate under the circumstances." (Fisher v. Larsen (1982) 138 Cal.App.3d 627, 640.) As to the punitive damages claim, triable issues of material fact exist. Determining whether an individual is a "managing agent" under Cal. Civ. Code sec. 3294(b) is "a question of fact for decision on a case-by-case basis." (White v. Ultramar, Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 563, 566-67.) The evidence establishes that a reasonable jury could find that Defendant Gloria Lau exercised "a substantial independent authority and judgment in [her] corporate decisionmaking so that [her] decisions ultimately determine corporate policy," given that the evidence establishes that Lau had the authority to email all nursing staff about the voicemail, as well as have Plaintiff's picture posted throughout the hospital. (UMF 111, 172.) Further, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the Vice President of Human Resource's ratified Lau's conduct, given that the Vice President knew that Lau sent the email, but did not send, or instruct Lau to send, a follow up email stating that Defendants were still investigating who left the voicemail. (UMF 178-179.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: EAST WEST BANK VS. AMIR SHAHMIRZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, September 10, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT AMIR SHAMIRZA's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend and Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff is precluded by CCP section 726 from bringing an action to recover against Defendant because Plaintiff has already begun pursuing a non-judicial foreclosure for the same secured property. The fact that Defendant personally guaranteed the obligation of APTELM LLC aka 537 ELM LLC does not permit Plaintiff to seek remedies for breach of the obligation because, as the 100% member interest holder of the LLC, Defendant was already liable on the guaranteed obligation. (See CC § 2787; Cadle Co. II v. Harvey (2000) 83 Cal.App.4th 927, 932.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EHG)

The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER HECTOR VS. GLORIA PEREIRA AKA GLO PERIERA AKA GLORY PERIERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production, Set One And Monetary Sacntions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, September 10, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER HECTOR Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production, Set One And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem issued the following ruling: Motion granted. Defendant to produce the requested documents without objection within 10 days. Sanctions to Plaintiff in the amount of $60. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
